annotations.label: Group (informal)

anchor.snippet: a group is a set with an operation that combines any two elements

annotations.note: The `Group` typeclass in Mathlib captures the informal notion of a set with an associative binary operation, identity, and inverses.

annotations.label: Integers under addition

anchor.snippet: One of the more familiar groups is the set of integers

annotations.note: Mathlib equips `ℤ` with `Int.instAddCommGroup`, giving the standard additive group structure (the previously cited `Int.instCommGroup` does not exist).

annotations.label: Group (formal definition)

anchor.snippet: A group is a set

annotations.note: `class Group (G : Type u) extends DivInvMonoid G` in `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.lean` is the direct formalization.

annotations.label: Identity element

anchor.snippet: It is called the identity element

annotations.note: The identity is carried by the `One` typeclass with axioms `one_mul`/`mul_one` from `MulOneClass`.

annotations.label: Inverse element

anchor.snippet: it is called the inverse of

annotations.note: Inverses are encoded by the `Inv` typeclass together with `inv_mul_cancel` in the `Group` class.

annotations.label: Abelian group

anchor.snippet: the operation is said to be commutative , and the group is called an abelian group

annotations.note: `class CommGroup extends Group, CommMonoid` (additive variant `AddCommGroup`).

annotations.label: Additive and multiplicative group notation

anchor.snippet: one speaks of an additive group whenever the group operation is notated as addition

annotations.note: Mathlib has parallel `Group`/`AddGroup` hierarchies linked via the `to_additive` attribute and `Additive`/`Multiplicative` type tags.

annotations.label: Dihedral group D4 (symmetries of a square)

anchor.snippet: These symmetries determine a group called the dihedral group of degree four

annotations.note: `DihedralGroup n` formalizes the dihedral group of order 2n; setting `n = 4` yields D4.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of identity element

anchor.snippet: The group axioms imply that the identity element is unique

annotations.note: Mathlib bakes the identity into the `One` typeclass so a separate uniqueness lemma is not stated, though the underlying calculation appears as `left_inv_eq_right_inv`.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of inverses

anchor.snippet: The group axioms also imply that the inverse of each element is unique

annotations.note: `left_inv_eq_right_inv` shows any left inverse equals any right inverse, giving uniqueness of inverses.

annotations.label: Unique solution of left equation (left translation is a bijection)

anchor.snippet: there is a unique solution

annotations.note: `mul_right_injective a` gives injectivity of left translation; combined with `mul_inv_cancel_left` this yields the unique solution `x = a⁻¹b`.

annotations.label: Unique solution of right equation (right translation is a bijection)

anchor.snippet: the unique solution

annotations.note: `mul_left_injective a` provides injectivity of right translation, dual to the left case.

annotations.label: Equivalent definition via one-sided axioms

anchor.snippet: The group axioms for identity and inverses may be "weakened" to assert only the existence of a left identity and left inverses

annotations.note: `Group.ofLeftAxioms` builds a `Group` from associativity, left identity, and left inverses; `Group.ofRightAxioms` is the mirror version.

annotations.label: Left identity + right inverse insufficient

anchor.snippet: only assuming the existence of a left identity and a right inverse (or vice versa) is not sufficient to define a group

annotations.note: No counterexample showing left-identity + right-inverse fails to give a group is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group homomorphism

anchor.snippet: Group homomorphisms

annotations.note: `MonoidHom M N` (notation `M →* N`) is Mathlib's bundled multiplicative homomorphism, used for group homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of groups

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism is a homomorphism that has an inverse homomorphism

annotations.note: `MulEquiv` (notation `≃*`) bundles a multiplicative bijection with its inverse, i.e. a group isomorphism.

annotations.label: Subgroup

anchor.snippet: Informally, a subgroup is a group

annotations.note: `structure Subgroup (G : Type*) [Group G] extends Submonoid G` in `Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Defs.lean`.

annotations.label: Subgroup test

anchor.snippet: The subgroup test provides a necessary and sufficient condition

annotations.note: The closure conditions are bundled into the `Subgroup` structure; no separate `subgroup_test` lemma in the one-step form `a*b⁻¹ ∈ H` was located.

annotations.label: Subgroup generated by a subset

anchor.snippet: the subgroup generated by

annotations.note: `Subgroup.closure k` is the minimal subgroup containing the set `k`.

annotations.label: Cosets and normal subgroup

anchor.snippet: a subgroup

annotations.note: `Subgroup.Normal` formalizes normal subgroups; cosets are formalized via `QuotientGroup.leftRel` in `Mathlib.GroupTheory.Coset.Defs`.

annotations.label: Left cosets form a partition

anchor.snippet: The left cosets of any subgroup

annotations.note: `QuotientGroup.leftRel H` is the setoid whose equivalence classes are the left cosets of H, yielding the partition.

annotations.label: Quotient group

anchor.snippet: is called a quotient group or factor group

annotations.note: `G ⧸ N` with `[N.Normal]` carries a `Group` instance built via `QuotientGroup.Quotient.group`.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem

anchor.snippet: The first isomorphism theorem implies that any surjective homomorphism

annotations.note: `quotientKerEquivOfSurjective` gives `G ⧸ ker φ ≃* H` for surjective `φ`.

annotations.label: Presentation of a group

anchor.snippet: This is called a presentation of

annotations.note: `PresentedGroup rels` is `FreeGroup α` modulo the normal closure of `rels`.

annotations.label: Every group is a quotient of a free group

anchor.snippet: Every group is isomorphic to a quotient of a free group

annotations.note: Mathlib has `FreeGroup.lift` and the surjection `FreeGroup G →* G`, from which the quotient statement follows, but no named lemma packaging the universal-quotient fact was located.

annotations.label: Fundamental group of plane minus a point

anchor.snippet: if the topological space is the plane with one point removed

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup` is defined but the specific computation π₁(ℝ²∖{0}) ≃ ℤ was not found.

annotations.label: Integers under multiplication not a group

anchor.snippet: The integers, with the operation of multiplication instead of addition

annotations.note: No explicit statement that `(ℤ, ·)` fails to be a group exists; Mathlib simply omits a `Group ℤ` instance under multiplication.

annotations.label: Nonzero rationals under multiplication

anchor.snippet: the set of all nonzero rational numbers

annotations.note: `ℚ` is a `Field` (via `Rat.instField`), so `ℚˣ` is a `CommGroup` under multiplication via the standard `Units` construction.

annotations.label: Modular addition group Z/nZ

anchor.snippet: Modular addition, defined in this way

annotations.note: `ZMod n` is the additive group ℤ/nℤ, with `Int.quotientZMultiplesNatEquivZMod` showing `ℤ ⧸ ⟨n⟩ ≃+ ZMod n`.

annotations.label: Multiplicative group of integers modulo a prime

anchor.snippet: there is also the multiplicative group of integers modulo

annotations.note: `(ZMod p)ˣ` is the unit group; `ZMod.unitsEquivCoprime` characterises units as coprime residues.

annotations.label: Cyclic group

anchor.snippet: A cyclic group is a group all of whose elements are powers of a particular element

annotations.note: `class IsCyclic` in `Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Defs` asserts every element is a power of a generator.

annotations.label: Group of nth roots of unity

anchor.snippet: the group of

annotations.note: `rootsOfUnity n M` is the subgroup of `Mˣ` of n-th roots of unity, with cyclicity proved over integral domains.

annotations.label: Infinite cyclic group is isomorphic to Z

anchor.snippet: An infinite cyclic group is isomorphic to

annotations.note: `intCyclicMulEquiv : Multiplicative ℤ ≃* G` realises the isomorphism for any infinite cyclic group.

annotations.label: Group action

anchor.snippet: A group is said to act on another mathematical object

annotations.note: `class MulAction (α β) [Monoid α]` formalizes group/monoid actions; specialised to a group it is the standard notion.

annotations.label: General linear group

anchor.section: General linear group and representation theory
anchor.snippet: The general linear group

annotations.note: Both `LinearMap.GeneralLinearGroup` and `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup` are defined; the matrix version is `GLₙ(R)`.

annotations.label: Linear representation of a group

anchor.section: General linear group and representation theory
anchor.snippet: A representation of a group

annotations.note: `abbrev Representation k G V := G →* (V →ₗ[k] V)` formalizes linear representations.

annotations.label: Galois group of the quadratic

anchor.snippet: the solutions of the quadratic equation

annotations.note: `Polynomial.Gal p` defines the Galois group of any polynomial; no decl specifically computes the quadratic case.

annotations.label: Finite group and order

anchor.snippet: A group is called finite if it has a finite number of elements

annotations.note: Finite groups are `[Group G] [Finite G]`; the order is `Nat.card G` (or `Fintype.card G`).

annotations.label: Symmetric group on n letters

anchor.snippet: An important class is the symmetric groups

annotations.note: `Equiv.Perm α` is the symmetric group; for `α = Fin n` it is the symmetric group on n letters.

annotations.label: Cayley's theorem

anchor.snippet: any finite group can be expressed as a subgroup of a symmetric group

annotations.note: `MulAction.toPermHom G G : G →* Equiv.Perm G` provides the Cayley embedding, but no named lemma asserting injectivity as 'Cayley's theorem' was located.

annotations.label: Order of an element

anchor.snippet: The order of an element

annotations.note: `orderOf x` is the smallest positive n with `x^n = 1` (or 0 if none), defined in `Mathlib.GroupTheory.OrderOfElement`.

annotations.label: Lagrange's theorem

anchor.snippet: Lagrange's Theorem states that for a finite group

annotations.note: `Subgroup.card_subgroup_dvd_card` states |H| divides |G| for a subgroup H of a finite group G.

annotations.label: Structure theorem for finite abelian groups

anchor.snippet: Any finite abelian group is isomorphic to a product of finite cyclic groups

annotations.note: Decomposes any finite abelian group as a direct sum of cyclic `ZMod (p^k)` groups.

annotations.label: Groups of prime order are cyclic

anchor.snippet: Any group of prime order

annotations.note: `isCyclic_of_prime_card` proves `IsCyclic G` whenever `Nat.card G` is prime.

annotations.label: Simple group

anchor.snippet: A nontrivial group is called simple if it has no such normal subgroup

annotations.note: `class IsSimpleGroup : Prop extends Nontrivial G` characterises simple groups via their normal-subgroup lattice.

annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups

anchor.snippet: The classification of all finite simple groups was a major achievement

annotations.note: The classification of finite simple groups is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Group via operations (equational definition)

anchor.snippet: An equivalent definition of group consists of replacing

annotations.note: `Group.ofLeftAxioms`/`Group.ofRightAxioms` build a `Group` from minimal equational axioms on `Mul`, `Inv`, `One`.

annotations.label: Topological group

anchor.snippet: Such groups are called topological groups

annotations.note: `class IsTopologicalGroup G` requires continuity of multiplication and inversion.

annotations.label: Lie group

anchor.snippet: A Lie group is a group that also has the structure of a differentiable manifold

annotations.note: `class LieGroup` formalizes a group with smooth multiplication and inversion on a manifold.

annotations.label: General linear group as a Lie group

anchor.snippet: A standard example is the general linear group introduced above

annotations.note: GLₙ is defined and the smooth-manifold/Lie-group machinery exists, but I did not locate an explicit `LieGroup` instance on `GL n ℝ`.

annotations.label: Monoid (relaxation)

anchor.snippet: if the requirement that every element has an inverse is eliminated, the resulting algebraic structure is called a monoid

annotations.note: `class Monoid extends Semigroup, MulOneClass` formalises monoids in `Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Defs`.

annotations.label: Groupoid

anchor.snippet: a groupoid is any small category in which every morphism is an isomorphism

annotations.note: `class Groupoid (obj : Type u) extends Category obj` with `inv` on every morphism.

annotations.label: n-ary group

anchor.snippet: this gives a notion of

annotations.note: No n-ary (polyadic) group structure was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Associativity

anchor.snippet: This property is known as associativity

annotations.note: Associativity of the group operation is captured by the `Semigroup` typeclass (axiom `mul_assoc`), which every `Group` extends.

annotations.label: Category of groups

anchor.snippet: form a category , the category of groups

annotations.note: `GrpCat` is the bundled category of groups with group homomorphisms; `AddGrpCat` is the additive analogue (the previously cited `Grp` is not the correct name).

annotations.label: Identity homomorphism

anchor.snippet: The identity homomorphism of a group

annotations.note: `MonoidHom.id G : G →* G` is the identity group homomorphism.

annotations.label: Free group

anchor.snippet: Every group is isomorphic to a quotient of a free group

annotations.note: `FreeGroup α` is the free group on the generators α, constructed as reduced words.

annotations.label: Cayley graph

anchor.snippet: the Cayley graph

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.mulCayley s` is the Cayley graph induced by a generating set `s` (with `SimpleGraph.addCayley` for the additive version).

annotations.label: Fundamental group

anchor.snippet: introducing the fundamental group

annotations.note: `FundamentalGroup X x` is the fundamental group of `X` based at `x`, derived from the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: Center of a group

anchor.snippet: such as center and commutator

annotations.note: `Subgroup.center G` is the subgroup of elements commuting with all of G.

annotations.label: Commutator subgroup

anchor.snippet: such as center and commutator

annotations.note: `commutator G` is the subgroup generated by all commutators `[g,h] = g h g⁻¹ h⁻¹`.

annotations.label: Sylow theorems

anchor.snippet: The Sylow theorems give a partial converse

annotations.note: `Sylow p G` and accompanying lemmas like `Sylow.exists_subgroup_card_pow_prime` formalize the existence/conjugacy/count Sylow theorems.

annotations.label: Jordan–Hölder theorem

anchor.snippet: in a sense made precise by the Jordan–Hölder theorem

annotations.note: `CompositionSeries.jordan_holder` proves uniqueness (up to permutation) of composition factors for any composition series.

annotations.label: Grothendieck group

anchor.snippet: Grothendieck group

annotations.note: The Grothendieck-group construction can be obtained as `Localization` of a commutative monoid (no dedicated `GroupCompletion` decl exists).

annotations.label: Special orthogonal group SO(n)

anchor.section: General linear group and representation theory
anchor.snippet: Another important matrix group is the special orthogonal group

annotations.note: `Matrix.specialOrthogonalGroup n R` is the subgroup of orthogonal matrices of determinant 1 (the orthogonal group itself is `Matrix.orthogonalGroup`).

annotations.label: Group object in a category

anchor.snippet: the notion of group object in a category

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes group objects in a cartesian monoidal category as the `CategoryTheory.GrpObj` typeclass (with bundled category `CategoryTheory.Grp`); `CategoryTheory.GroupObject` is not a real name.
